channel.title: Latest Updates
channel.description: The most recent blogs and online articles from The Economist

item.title: AI super-apps are remaking Chinaâs internet
item.description: Welcome to the agentic era

item.title: Russia is starting to lose ground in Ukraine
item.description: Our tracker suggests it has suffered its first sustained net loss since October 2023

item.title: The battle to lead Labourâand Britainâhangs on a by-election
item.description: If Andy Burnham beats Reform UK in June he will ride into Westminster a hero

item.title: Indiaâs loudest political fight obscures a more urgent one
item.description: The countryâs collapsing cities could benefit from plans to redraw the electoral map

item.title: Checks and Balance newsletter: A fix for Donald Trumpâs jobs problem
item.description: Charlotte Howard, our New York bureau chief, explains how the president can solve the decline in working men

item.title: PlotÂ TwistÂ newsletter:Â This self-help book has hit the zeitgeist

item.description: How we chose this weekâs images

item.title: The Curious Case of the Missing Milk Supply
item.description: Why the science of breastfeeding is in its infancy

item.title: Who is leading the race to replace Sir Keir Starmer?
item.description: We are tracking the odds for Labourâs leadership contest

item.title: How well do anabolic steroids work?
item.description: Very. But beware the side-effects

item.title: Andy Burnham, Britainâs could-be prime minister, is a man of two parts
item.description: 50% Manchester, 50% Merseyside

item.title: What did Trump and Xi actually achieve?
item.description: Divergent accounts suggest little progress on trade, Taiwan, Iran and AI

item.title: The complex masculinity of UFC
item.description: Our weekly podcast on America. This week, Donald Trumpâs patronage of the Ultimate Fighting Championship

item.title: Why the sex in âRivalsâ is more than mere titillation
item.description: In the hit TV drama, back for a second season, a bonk is never just a bonk

item.title: Is British politics broken?
item.description: Readers sent us their thoughts on the turmoil that has engulfed Sir Keir Starmer and the Labour Party

item.title: What to watch this week
item.description: Four recommendations that are sure to please

item.description: The countryâs most famous photojournalist died on April 26th, aged 83

item.description: Medicine has neglected mammary glands for too long
